pattern: Greedily filling each cell with as much as its row and column can still spare, left to right and top to bottom, always leaves enough remaining capacity to satisfy every later row and column exactly, so no backtracking is ever needed.
hints: Many different matrices can satisfy the same row and column sums, so there's no single 'correct' matrix to reconstruct, only a valid one.
Fill the matrix greedily: for each cell, put in as much as the smaller of its row's remaining budget and its column's remaining budget allows.

Subtract what you placed from both that row's and that column's remaining totals, then move to the next cell; by the time you reach the last cell of each row and column, its remaining budget is guaranteed to fit exactly.

edge_inputs.description: a three-by-three reconstruction with an uneven split
a single row and single column, forced to one value
all sums are zero, the only valid matrix is all zeros
a row of all zeros next to a nonzero row must still balance columns

stress.description: random row/column sums with matching totals at larger scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, min(size, 500))
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 500))
    mat = [[rng.randint(0, 20) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    row_sum = [sum(row) for row in mat]
    col_sum = [sum(mat[i][j] for i in range(m)) for j in range(n)]
    return [row_sum, col_sum]
